full_response:
The product of two \(3 \times 3\) matrices \([M]\) and \([N]\) is given in summation notation as:

\[
([M] \cdot [N])_{ij} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{ik} N_{kj}, \quad i, j = 1, 2, 3.
\]

This means each element of the resulting matrix is the sum of products of corresponding entries from the \(i\)-th row of \([M]\) and the \(j\)-th column of \([N]\).

\boxed{([M]\cdot [N])_{ij} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{ik} N_{kj}}

Confidence: 100%
